name: Curio Desktop-First Monolith — "one app, files are the API"
summary: Curio is a single signed-and-notarized Tauri 2 desktop app (macOS/Windows/Linux) with zero server components ever: a Cargo workspace of seven pure Rust crates under a thin Tauri shell, a SvelteKit/Svelte 5 runes frontend driven entirely by generated IPC bindings, and one SQLite file (WAL, writer-actor + read pool, event-sourced user state) as the only runtime store. Every integration surface is a file the user owns: OPML for subscriptions, a generic templated markdown-vault exporter for promoted articles, an append-only JSONL reading-event log for behavior signals, and a one-command full-portability archive. This is the winning shape for OSS because the 2026 market seat — a healthy, cross-platform, power-user desktop reader — is literally empty (NetNewsWire is Apple-only, Fluent Reader is dead Electron, Folo is a closed backend), and a tiny team can actually sustain it: no backend to operate, no sync protocol to version against a fleet of clients, no account system, and a crate layout where ~90% of the code is `cargo test`-able without a webview. The trust story ("SQLite you can open, markdown you keep, JSONL you can tail, no telemetry, can't be acquihired away") is simultaneously the post-Omnivore marketing line and the Knowledge Plane ingestion contract — the KP consumes Curio's files, not its process, so Curio's DB stays derived/disposable per RFC-0018's canonical/derived rule.

key_decisions.decision: Single-process Tauri monolith over a Cargo workspace of seven pure crates; the src-tauri shell contains zero logic
key_decisions.rationale: For a tiny OSS team the maintenance surface IS the architecture: no server, no protocol versioning, one release artifact per OS. The crate split makes ~90% of the code testable with plain `cargo test` (no webview toolchain — Tauri's Linux dev-env pain is the #1 contributor deterrent), enforces the brief's clean layering by compiler rather than convention, and leaves a headless CLI/MCP binary as a cheap post-v1 addition without designing it in now.

key_decisions.decision: SQLite in WAL mode behind a writer-actor (one owning thread, channel-fed, all mutations transactional with INSERT..ON CONFLICT) plus a read-connection pool accessed via spawn_blocking
key_decisions.rationale: Kills the branch's fatal storage model (sync Mutex<Connection> on tokio workers, 2N-statement untransacted upserts, N fsyncs per refresh). A single writer matches SQLite's actual concurrency model, WAL lets reads proceed during refresh writes (no UI freeze at 50k articles), and an actor is far simpler for contributors than a generic async-SQL framework. No sqlx/ORM: boring, auditable SQL.

key_decisions.decision: User state is event-sourced: an append-only `events` table (ULID id, type, article ref, ts, payload) is written first, and read/star/read-later flags are materialized into `article_state` in the same transaction
key_decisions.rationale: One mechanism yields three products: correct undo-able UI state, the JSONL reading-signals export (a cursor-tailing checkpointer, near-free), and the substrate for future file-based multi-device merge (union of append-only logs + LWW by timestamp — no CRDT library). Separating state from `articles` also fixes retention (reaper never touches stated articles) and stops FTS-trigger churn on is_read flips.

key_decisions.decision: Schema fixes locked at v1: articles get INTEGER PRIMARY KEY (UUIDv7 as a UNIQUE column), FTS5 external-content keyed to the real integer PK, all timestamps INTEGER unix-millis, dedup chain guid→link→hash(title+published), source_updated_at split from row modified_at
key_decisions.rationale: The branch's FTS is keyed to implicit rowids of a TEXT-PK table — silently corrupted by VACUUM; UUIDv4 TEXT keys wreck insert locality at 100k rows; datetime('now') vs RFC3339 strings break ORDER BY today. These are near-immutable once real user DBs exist, so they are day-one decisions, not refactors.

key_decisions.decision: IPC boundary is 100% generated: tauri-specta emits TypeScript commands, types, and event bindings; committed output is CI diff-gated; hand-written invoke() calls are lint-banned
key_decisions.rationale: The branch's app is broken at runtime by a snake_case/camelCase arg mismatch and an error-shape drift that 139 green tests never touched. Codegen makes that bug class unrepresentable, gives the frontend a typed event channel for cache invalidation, and replaces the ts-rs scaffolding theater with something that demonstrably runs in CI.

key_decisions.decision: Defense-in-depth rendering: ammonia sanitization at INGEST (stored HTML is already clean), strict CSP (default-src 'self', frame-src youtube-nocookie.com only), article HTML rendered inside a sandboxed srcdoc iframe, minimal capabilities file, shell plugin removed, opener plugin URL-scoped, and export/import paths accepted only from dialog-plugin picks or registered vault roots — never raw IPC strings
key_decisions.rationale: The branch ships a complete stored-XSS→arbitrary-file-write chain (raw feed HTML + {@html} + csp:null + unscoped export commands). An RSS reader's core job is rendering hostile input inside a webview that holds file-writing IPC; any single defense failing must not be sufficient for compromise. This is also the credibility bar for a security-positioned OSS launch.

key_decisions.decision: One fetch pipeline: the (fixed) RefreshService pattern is the only refresh path — shared reqwest client with timeouts/size caps/redirect caps, bounded buffer_unordered, per-host politeness, SSRF guard denying private ranges post-DNS, conditional GET state preserved across errors, feed-health FSM with dead-feed autopause and 301 URL rewriting; manual refresh enqueues into the same scheduler
key_decisions.rationale: The branch has two divergent refresh implementations — the good one dead, the shipped one sequential and untimed (one hung feed wedges the app). Polite fetching at 500+ feeds is table stakes, SSRF policy is mandatory for an app whose function is fetching arbitrary URLs, and a single pipeline halves the code a tiny team maintains.

key_decisions.decision: v1 ships rich view-mode LAYOUTS (reddit/youtube/podcast card+grid rendering of RSS-native data, open-in-browser, youtube-nocookie click-to-load facades) but NO unofficial-endpoint enrichment; Reddit JSON and yt-dlp live behind Enricher traits as feature-flagged optional v1.x providers; yt-dlp is never bundled — PATH-detected or managed-download with pinned version + SHA256 into the data dir, `--`-separated argv, URL validation
key_decisions.rationale: Bundled auto-updating binaries break macOS notarization, Flathub, and distro policy; unauthenticated Reddit JSON is throttled to ~10 req/min and ToS-gray; yt-dlp argv currently permits flag injection (--exec → RCE). The trait seam keeps the differentiator alive without chaining launch stability to the two highest-breakage third-party dependencies in the design.

key_decisions.decision: Frontend is Svelte 5 runes with a thin query-cache whose invalidation is driven by Rust-emitted events; the backend owns ALL counts, filtering, sorting, and keyset pagination; the article list is virtualized
key_decisions.rationale: The branch's store layer computes unread badges from the currently-loaded 50-article page (wrong by construction), double-filters, and refetches the folder tree per article click. Single-source-of-truth-in-Rust is cheaper to keep correct at 50k articles and makes component tests honest (mockIPC against generated bindings).

key_decisions.decision: Release engineering is v1 core scope: Developer ID signing + notarization, Windows signing, tauri-plugin-updater with signed manifests (disabled in deb/rpm/Flatpak builds), conditional signing so fork PRs pass CI, SHA-pinned actions, cargo-deny enforced, single version source, human-gated publish
key_decisions.rationale: The dossier's clearest production gap: unsigned DMGs are Gatekeeper-blocked, the current release workflow has vestigial signing env vars and auto-publishes drafts, and updater identifiers are near-immutable after first release. Ship the pipeline with the first alpha so it hardens with the product — retrofitting signing/updater identity is the one mistake you can't squash-merge away.

key_decisions.decision: Testing pyramid matched to layers: proptest + unit in pure crates (dedup keys, filename/slug sanitization, OPML round-trip); golden-file corpus tests for parsing/extraction/markdown (fixtures/ nasty corpus, licensing-scrubbed); rusqlite integration tests incl. migration-from-every-released-DB; wiremock tests for the fetch pipeline; vitest browser-mode E2E against mockIPC using generated bindings; tauri-driver smoke on Linux+Windows CI; documented manual smoke matrix per OS per release; cargo-fuzz on feed parse + HTML→MD; criterion benches + CI cold-start budget against the 50k-article synthetic fixture
key_decisions.rationale: The branch's 139 tests validated easy code and missed every load-bearing failure (dead FTS, IPC arg bug, vanishing folder subtrees, multi-line markdown destruction). Each test tier here is chosen to be implementable as specified — no Playwright-drives-Tauri fiction — and the corpus + fixture-DB approach converts the two riskiest surfaces (real-world feeds, schema migrations) into regression floors.

key_decisions.decision: Storage layout per platform via Tauri path resolver: config (settings/themes JSON) vs data (curio.db, vault manifests, downloads) vs cache (content-hash image store, LRU-evicted, size-capped)
key_decisions.rationale: The branch puts a multi-GB cache in ~/.config — breaks XDG, backup tools, and roaming profiles. Documented file locations are also part of the data-portability trust story.

key_decisions.decision: Governance shipped as product: dual MIT/Apache-2.0, PRIVACY.md enumerating every possible network call, SECURITY.md + disclosure channel, GOVERNANCE.md stating 'personal-first, community-welcome' (the KP use case drives the roadmap, honestly), DCO not CLA, name/trademark clearance + identifier fix before any public artifact
key_decisions.rationale: Post-Omnivore/Pocket, 'structurally cannot be taken away' is a rational selection criterion for exactly this audience, and the dossier flags 'Curio' name collision + the .app identifier as launch-blocking. These are cheap now and reputation-expensive later.

kp_seam: The seam is one crate (curio-export) exposing two generic, versioned, file-based contracts — nothing in the product names the Knowledge Plane. (1) VAULT EXPORT: a "vault" is any user-registered directory (name, root, minijinja template, optional per-feed/per-tag routing rules) — Obsidian is a docs example, not a code path. Promotion (one keystroke in the reader, batch from read-later) runs the real content pipeline (sanitized HTML → readability → DOM-based htmd CommonMark) and writes atomically (tmp+rename, safe for git-watched vaults) a note with schema-versioned YAML frontmatter: curio_id (UUIDv7), source URL, feed, title, author, published/saved timestamps, tags, and content_checksum (sha256 of the normalized body) — the exact checksum-keyed dedupe/incremental-reindex contract RFC-0018's ingestion plane specifies. A per-vault manifest (.curio/manifest.json inside the vault) maps curio_id → relative path + checksum, making re-export idempotent (update-in-place, never duplicate), giving deterministic slug collision handling, and — because the manifest lives IN the vault — letting a wiped-and-reinstalled Curio reconcile against an existing vault instead of re-duplicating it. Images copy vault-relative under an assets dir with content-hash names. (2) EVENT LOG: a checkpointing tailer over the internal append-only events table writes JSONL to a configurable sink (default: data dir; pointable at a synced folder or the vault's .curio/events/), daily-rotated files of {event_id ULID, schema_version, type: subscribed|read|unread|starred|unstarred|read_later_added|read_later_removed|promoted, ts, article: {curio_id, url, title, feed_url, tags}}. Both schemas are published in docs/schemas/ as versioned public API with a documented breaking-change policy — they become load-bearing the moment one external consumer exists, and the KP is consumer #1. KP integration then requires zero Curio code: the KP ingestion plane (G11-side, per RFC-0018) watches the vault directory it already git-mirrors — promoted notes arrive as normalized markdown matching its frontmatter needs — and the librarian's interest model tails the JSONL for read/star/read-later signals. Curio's SQLite stays derived/disposable under the canonical/derived rule: promotion carries everything downstream needs, annotations land in the vault (never the app DB), and DB loss costs only unpromoted local state. The same two contracts are the OSS flagship feature ("your reading becomes your notes, on disk, in a format that outlives this app") for Logseq/plain-git/quantified-self users — the KP is just one subscriber.
sync_story: Honest v1: Curio is single-device-authoritative for feed/read state, and sync happens through files the user already syncs. What v1 SHIPS: (a) OPML import+export with the curio:* extension namespace — subscriptions move between machines losslessly; (b) vault export — promoted knowledge is durable and multi-device by whatever syncs the vault (git, Syncthing, iCloud Drive), readable on mobile today via Obsidian Mobile/any markdown app, which covers the KP couch-reach requirement without Curio touching mobile; (c) the JSONL event log, which can ride a synced folder; (d) a one-command full-portability archive (OPML + article state as events JSONL + settings + read-later) so a second machine can be seeded and no user is ever locked in. What v1 does NOT do — stated plainly: no live multi-device read-state sync, no mobile app, no GReader/Fever client mode (that's the sync-service-client architecture, a different candidate), no Curio server ever. The designed-but-not-shipped v1.x path: because user state is event-sourced with ULID ids and timestamps, per-device append-only event journals written into a user-synced folder merge by pure set-union of event ids plus last-writer-wins per (article, state-dimension) on timestamp — an additive feature over the existing events table, no schema migration, no CRDT dependency, no server. That serves the multi-desktop power user (Syncthing/iCloud crowd); it still does not produce a phone app, and the architecture does not pretend otherwise — the mobile answer is the vault plus the portability archive, full stop.
code_reuse: Roughly 35-40% of the branch survives, mostly at the edges. SURVIVES AS-IS or with trivial fixes: error.rs three-tier taxonomy (verbatim, into curio-core; add serde tag attr so the wire shape is flat); serde model layer (into curio-core; delete the duplicate Theme structs in models/settings.rs); the ammonia sanitizer config (into curio-content, finally wired at ingest; fix byte-slice truncation); the quick-xml OPML state machine with curio:* round-trip (into curio-opml; fix O(n²) import + namespace declaration); feed-rs parser usage + its RSS/Atom tests (into curio-fetch; populate content_text, fix site_url/published fallbacks); YouTube/Reddit URL parsers with their genuinely thorough tests (into curio-enrich); themes.rs data (moved to JSON assets); thin command-wrapper shape from commands/articles.rs. SURVIVES AS DESIGN REFERENCE: the SQLite schema (FKs, FTS5 external-content protocol, indexes) seeds curio-store's migration 0001 after the rowid/UUIDv7/timestamp/article_state surgery; services/refresh.rs is the reference implementation for curio-fetch's pipeline (its bounded-concurrency + conditional-GET structure is right, its wiring and error paths are not). FRONTEND: app.css token system + 9 themes carry forward nearly verbatim; AddFeedModal's dialog a11y and error-unwrapping patterns; Reddit/YouTube viewer components kept as visual reference for the deferred enrichment views; format.ts + its 36 tests. DX LAYER (~70% survives): Makefile, lefthook.yml, .vscode, biome/tsconfig/svelte.config, setup.sh, CI skeleton including the Cfqby branch's clippy-needs-frontend-prebuild fix and its gen/-trim + real icons (cherry-picked ideas, not merged — it forked before HEAD's last commits). REWRITTEN: storage/database.rs (writer-actor repository), commands/feeds.rs refresh path (collapses into curio-fetch), core/content/markdown.rs (regex converter discarded for htmd/DOM), commands/export.rs (becomes curio-export), all frontend stores + the +page.svelte shell + api/index.ts (generated bindings), tauri.conf security posture, gen-types.sh (tauri-specta). DROPPED: platform/podcast dead structs, player.ts, favicon.rs implementation (strategy kept, Google fallback opt-in), the 34MB committed yt-dlp binary, committed gen/, unused deps (mockall/tokio-test/image; htmd stops being unused by being used).
risks: Mobile/multi-device read-state is the #1 churn driver for desktop-only readers, and this candidate's honest answer (vault + portability archive + future file-journal merge) is structurally weaker than a GReader-client-mode architecture — power users with existing FreshRSS/Miniflux setups may bounce; mitigation is messaging plus the events-table design keeping client-mode addable later, but it IS a bet against the table-stakes list.

Event-sourced state adds write-path and mental complexity versus plain flags; if the materialization or the v1.x journal-merge semantics (clock-skew LWW, deleted-feed references, event compaction as the log grows unboundedly) are gotten wrong, the flagship 'signals' contract ships bugs into a published schema that then requires a breaking version.

The exported frontmatter and events schemas become public API at v1 with at least one real consumer (the KP); schema mistakes discovered post-launch carry a compatibility tax a tiny team will feel — invest in the schema review before first release, not after.

Vault manifest and event files living inside the user's vault (.curio/) can conflict if the user runs Curio on two machines against one synced vault before journal-merge ships — needs explicit single-writer guidance in docs and a manifest-conflict recovery path, or it generates the project's first angry issues.

Deferred Reddit/yt-dlp enrichment is the demand magnet (Folo's 36k stars are for rich views); shipping v1 with layouts-only risks 'just another RSS reader' reception, while shipping enrichment later re-opens the ToS/breakage treadmill the deferral avoided — the trait seam contains but does not eliminate this tension.

Dependency concentration on Tauri 2 + tauri-specta + WebKitGTK: Tauri major-version churn is real, specta is a smaller-community project on the critical codegen path, and Linux WebKitGTK brings rendering/a11y/GStreamer-audio variance that no amount of Rust-side testing catches — the tauri-driver smoke tier and manual matrix are load-bearing, not optional.

Signing costs and ceremony (Apple Developer ID + notarization, Windows cert/SmartScreen reputation cold-start) are recurring real-money and key-custody obligations for an OSS project; the updater private key is a single point of catastrophic compromise and needs a custody/rotation plan before the first signed release.

Performance promises (instant search at 100k items, <1s first view at 50k articles) are the marketing line and currently unproven on WebKitGTK-class hardware; the criterion + cold-start CI gates must exist from the first alpha or the claims rot into the same unfalsifiable metrics the PRD had.

'Curio' trademark collision and the com.curio-reader.app identifier must be resolved before ANY public artifact — the identifier is baked into updater/signing identity and is effectively immutable afterward.

Tiny-team bus factor: the architecture minimizes ops but the corpus maintenance (feeds break in new ways continuously), fixture licensing hygiene, and per-OS release QA are ongoing human costs; GOVERNANCE.md's documented-release-process mitigation only works if it is actually written and rehearsed.

name: core-plus-heads
summary: Curio becomes a Cargo workspace whose product is `curio-core` — a Tauri-free Rust library crate owning the entire reading engine: policy-hardened fetching (SSRF guard, size/redirect caps, conditional GET), sanitize-at-ingest content pipeline (ammonia → readability → text extraction → htmd DOM-walk Markdown), pooled WAL SQLite with core-owned migrations, an event-sourced article-state model, feature-flagged enrichment providers (Reddit JSON, yt-dlp) behind trait ports, and the two strategic seams as first-class core modules: a generic markdown-vault exporter with an idempotency manifest, and an append-only reading-behavior event log with a versioned published schema. Heads are thin adapters: `curio-desktop` (Tauri 2 + Svelte 5 runes, IPC types generated by tauri-specta so the snake_case/error-drift bug class is impossible by construction) is the v1 head; `curio-cli` ships in v1 as both the agent/cron/scripting surface and the CI proof that the core runs headless; `curio-server` (axum daemon exposing GReader/Fever + REST over the same crate) is a designed-but-unbuilt second head. This boundary is the open-source pitch: the hard 20% of an RSS reader (fetch politeness, real-world feed parsing, dedup, extraction, FTS) becomes an embeddable, scriptable, headless-CI-testable crate that other projects — TUI readers, MCP servers, the papr-style agent niche, and Alex's Knowledge Plane — can build on without adopting the GUI, and it resolves the mobile/server tension by making both future answers (a server head speaking GReader to the existing OSS mobile-client ecosystem, or a sync-client adapter into FreshRSS/Miniflux) cheap ports against proven seams rather than v1 commitments.
workspace_layout:
curio/                                  # one repo: Cargo workspace + npm workspace; dual MIT/Apache-2.0
├── Cargo.toml                          # [workspace], shared lints (clippy::unwrap_used deny outside tests), deny.toml
├── crates/
│   ├── curio-core/                     # THE PRODUCT — no tauri/UI deps (cargo-deny bans them in this tree)
│   │   ├── src/
│   │   │   ├── error.rs                # CoreError/InfraError — ported near-verbatim from branch
│   │   │   ├── model/                  # Feed, Folder, Article, ArticleState, Tag, Settings; serde + schemars
│   │   │   ├── store/                  # SqliteStore: WAL+busy_timeout, dedicated writer thread + read pool,
│   │   │   │   ├── migrations/         #   numbered embedded SQL, one tx per migration, forward-only
│   │   │   │   ├── repo.rs             #   async facade (spawn_blocking inside; heads never see rusqlite)
│   │   │   │   └── search.rs           #   FTS5 query builder — escapes/compiles user input, never raw MATCH
│   │   │   ├── fetch/                  # reqwest policy layer: shared client, timeouts, SSRF/private-IP policy,
│   │   │   │                           #   size caps, redirect caps, ETag/If-Modified-Since, permanent-redirect updates
│   │   │   ├── ingest/                 # feed-rs parse → normalize (dedupe chain, date fallbacks, xml:base)
│   │   │   │                           #   → ammonia sanitize (ported config) → readability → content_text extraction
│   │   │   ├── pipeline/               # refresh engine: bounded buffer_unordered, per-feed backoff, feed health,
│   │   │   │                           #   scheduler, batch tx upserts (INSERT..ON CONFLICT), progress events
│   │   │   ├── enrich/                 # trait Enricher port; providers behind cargo features:
│   │   │   │   ├── reddit/             #   public JSON, circuit breaker, typed structs (feature "reddit")
│   │   │   │   └── youtube/            #   yt-dlp EXTERNAL binary mgr: SHA256-pinned, argv-hardened (feature "ytdlp")
│   │   │   ├── export/                 # generic vault exporter: htmd conversion, minijinja templates,
│   │   │   │                           #   manifest-backed idempotency, atomic tmp+rename, path sanitization
│   │   │   ├── events/                 # append-only events table + JSONL projection, schema_version stamped
│   │   │   ├── opml/                   # curio:* extended OPML round-trip — ported verbatim
│   │   │   └── query.rs                # typed reads: full ArticleFilter honored, keyset pagination, SQL-owned counts
│   │   └── tests/                      # wiremock pipeline integration, corpus runner, proptest, fixture DBs
│   ├── curio-types/                    # DTO + event/frontmatter schema crate; specta derives; schemars → JSON Schema
│   │                                   #   artifacts published per release (the KP-facing public API surface)
│   ├── curio-cli/                      # `curio` bin: add/fetch/query/search/export/events tail/opml — v1 head,
│   │                                   #   doubles as headless E2E harness in CI and the agent/cron surface
│   └── curio-server/                   # OPTIONAL post-v1 head: axum; GReader+Fever server API + REST; web UI later
├── apps/desktop/                       # curio-desktop — first head, deliberately thin
│   ├── src-tauri/
│   │   ├── src/commands/               # ~40 thin async fns delegating to Arc<CurioCore>; zero business logic
│   │   ├── src/state.rs                # AppState { core }, settings loaded at startup
│   │   ├── capabilities/               # explicit per-window ACLs (no shell plugin; opener w/ URL scope; dialog for paths)
│   │   └── tauri.conf.json             # strict CSP, fixed identifier, updater artifacts, externalBin absent (no yt-dlp bundling)
│   └── src/
│       ├── lib/bindings/               # GENERATED by tauri-specta — committed, CI-diffed, hand-edit = build failure
│       ├── lib/state/                  # Svelte 5 runes stores; backend-owned unread counts; query-cache layer
│       ├── lib/components/             # sidebar/list(virtualized)/reader(view-mode dispatch)/viewers — ported visuals
│       └── routes/                     # shell: shortcut registry, modals, toast outlet, resizable panes
├── fixtures/                           # nasty-feed corpus (synthesized/scrubbed), seeded 1000-feed/50k-article DB generator
├── xtask/                              # cargo xtask: gen-bindings, gen-schemas, corpus, bench, dist
├── docs/                               # architecture, vault+event integration guide, PRIVACY.md, SECURITY.md, per-OS install
└── .github/workflows/                  # ci.yml (core headless tests+corpus+bench budget), desktop.yml (3-OS tauri build),
                                        # release.yml (sign/notarize conditional on secrets), security.yml (cargo-deny ENFORCED)

key_decisions.decision: curio-core is a Tauri-free library crate and the product; every head (desktop, CLI, optional server) is a thin adapter over the same Arc<CurioCore> service object
key_decisions.rationale: The 2026 landscape shows the empty seat is a maintained cross-platform power reader, and the emerging demand (papr, Folo MCP) is agent-drivable feeds. A headless crate is embeddable (other OSS readers/TUIs/MCP servers reuse it), scriptable (cron `curio fetch`), and CI-testable without a webview — which is also what makes the test pyramid honest: the risky code (fetch/parse/store/export) runs under plain `cargo test`. It structurally resolves the mobile/server tension: both futures are new heads, not rewrites, and v1 commits to neither.

key_decisions.decision: Ship curio-cli in v1 alongside the desktop head; curio-server exists in the workspace as a designed, feature-gated, explicitly-unshipped head
key_decisions.rationale: The CLI is cheap (thin over core), is the daily CI proof that the core boundary holds (any Tauri leakage breaks the headless build), serves the agent/quantified-self audience no incumbent serves, and gives the Knowledge Plane a scriptable surface (`curio export --vault`, `curio events tail`) from day one. The server head staying unbuilt keeps v1 honest to the PRD's no-server non-goal while making 'others can build on it' literal — the API seam is the core crate itself.

key_decisions.decision: Sanitize-at-ingest: the pipeline stores only ammonia-sanitized HTML plus extracted content_text; raw feed HTML never reaches the DB, and the webview gets a strict CSP + capabilities as defense-in-depth, not primary defense
key_decisions.rationale: Kills the branch's critical stored-XSS chain (raw HTML → {@html} → IPC file-write primitive) at the root and fixes FTS being a silent no-op (content_text finally populated) in the same pass. The ported ammonia config is good; it was just never called. CSP (default-src 'self'; frame-src youtube-nocookie.com; connect-src ipc:), a real capabilities/ dir, no shell plugin, and opener-with-URL-scope make a sanitizer miss non-catastrophic.

key_decisions.decision: Storage: core-owned numbered migrations (transactional, forward-only, pre-migration backup); articles get an INTEGER PRIMARY KEY rowid alias + UUIDv7 unique column; user state (read/starred/read-later + positions) moves out of articles into an event-sourced article_state projection; dedupe chain guid → link → hash(title+published); WAL + dedicated writer thread + read pool behind an async repo facade
key_decisions.rationale: Fixes every scale defect the assessment found without discarding the good schema: stable FTS5 external-content rowid mapping (TEXT-PK implicit rowids corrupt across VACUUM), insert locality at 100k articles, no FTS churn on is_read flips (state lives elsewhere), batch INSERT..ON CONFLICT in one tx replaces 2N-statement upserts, and readers never block behind a refresh. Heads never see rusqlite — the facade is the one place async-over-sync is handled, so the blocking-in-async class of bug can't recur in command code.

key_decisions.decision: IPC type safety by construction: tauri-specta generates the TS bindings and error types from the Rust command signatures; bindings are committed and CI-diffed; hand-written invoke() calls are lint-banned
key_decisions.rationale: The branch's fatal snake_case-vs-camelCase bug and the CommandError shape drift were invisible to a fully green test suite because types were hand-duplicated. Generation makes the entire bug class unrepresentable, and the ported three-tier error model (kept verbatim) serializes through the same generated contract. curio-types + schemars additionally publishes the vault-frontmatter and event schemas as versioned JSON Schema artifacts — the external-consumer contract.

key_decisions.decision: Enrichment is out-of-core-path, feature-flagged, and circuit-broken: trait Enricher port in core; Reddit public-JSON and yt-dlp providers as optional cargo features; yt-dlp is NEVER bundled or auto-updated — external binary discovered on PATH or downloaded on explicit user action with pinned version + SHA256 verification into the data dir, argv-hardened (`--` separator, strict YouTube-URL validation, no bare PATH fallback)
key_decisions.rationale: yt-dlp bundling breaks notarization, Flathub, and distro norms, and is a supply-chain hole (the branch commits a 33MB binary and downloads without checksums); Reddit's unauthenticated JSON is ToS-gray and throttled far below the PRD's stale numbers. Trait ports mean the reader is complete without enrichment (degrade-to-RSS with honest UI), breakage is a provider outage not a product outage, and the ToS-risky code can even be omitted from a distro build entirely.

key_decisions.decision: The vault exporter and the event log are core modules, not desktop features — generic 'any directory of Markdown + YAML' and 'JSONL signal stream', with idempotency via a manifest keyed on stable IDs + content checksums
key_decisions.rationale: This is simultaneously the strongest OSS differentiator (the $120/yr Readwise→Obsidian pipeline as a free, local, deterministic contract) and the Knowledge-Plane requirement. Living in core means the CLI, the desktop, and any future server head all export identically, and the contract is testable headlessly. The branch's regex Markdown converter — the exact seam KP depends on — is rebuilt on htmd (already in Cargo.toml, never used) with a real-world fixture suite.

key_decisions.decision: Article state is event-sourced: an append-only events table (subscribed/read/starred/read_later/promoted, ts, article identity) is the write path; current state is a projection; the JSONL export is a filtered replay
key_decisions.rationale: One mechanism serves three masters: the KP interest-model signal export falls out for free (the grain-of-salt pass predicted exactly this), retention/pruning can never orphan state, and it is the substrate that makes any future sync (server head, GReader client mode, or file-based journal sync) a deterministic merge problem instead of a retrofit.

key_decisions.decision: Frontend is a Svelte 5 runes rewrite of the state/shell layer on top of the kept CSS token system: generated bindings, backend-owned counts and filtering (no client re-filter/re-sort), virtualized lists, keyset pagination, a shortcut registry, and view-mode dispatch in the reader
key_decisions.rationale: The branch's store layer is Svelte-4-idiom with wrong badge math, double filtering, and frozen pagination — cheaper to rewrite against the generated API than to repair. The app.css token system + 9 themes is genuinely production-grade and carries the PRD's theming story at v1 scope (light/dark/system + tokens; editor deferred); the substantial Reddit/YouTube viewer components port as visual reference once dispatch exists.

key_decisions.decision: Testing architecture by layer: core = unit + proptest (dates, slugs, dedupe keys, truncation/UTF-8) + wiremock pipeline integration + a nasty-feed corpus runner + cargo-fuzz on parse, with a ~90% coverage gate on core only; desktop = vitest component tests against mockIPC with specta-generated contracts, tauri-driver smoke on Linux/Windows CI, documented manual per-OS release matrix; perf = seeded 1000-feed/50k-article fixture DB + criterion benches + startup-budget check in CI
key_decisions.rationale: Inverts the branch's test theater (139 fast tests covering the easy code, zero coverage of IPC, refresh, FTS, export). Gating coverage only where it is cheap and meaningful (the pure core) avoids GUI-coverage fiction; the corpus runner makes feed-parsing robustness a regression suite instead of a hope; Playwright-drives-Tauri is dropped as unimplementable per the critique.

key_decisions.decision: Performance contract: batch transactional upserts, bounded-concurrency refresh (the unwired refresh.rs design, fixed and made the ONLY fetch path), FTS5 with populated content, keyset pagination everywhere, counts computed in SQL, image cache in XDG cache dir with enforced LRU caps — all verified against the seeded fixture DB in CI on fixed runners
key_decisions.rationale: The 500-feed/50k-article targets are currently unfalsifiable claims; making the dataset generator and benches part of the workspace turns '<1s first view, instant search at 100k' into a public, regression-gated benchmark — which the landscape analysis identifies as a marketable differentiator against Electron competitors.

key_decisions.decision: OSS/production hygiene decided now, not later: dual MIT/Apache-2.0 with cargo-deny license+advisory enforcement, DCO, SECURITY.md + PRIVACY.md enumerating every network call class, signing/notarization in the release workflow from the first alpha (conditional on secrets so fork PRs pass), reproducible-ish builds with checksummed artifacts, no binaries or generated dirs in git, name/identifier clearance before any public artifact
key_decisions.rationale: Post-Omnivore/Pocket, 'structurally can't be taken away' is the purchasing criterion for this audience; trust artifacts are features. The Tauri identifier and signing keys are near-immutable after first release, so these are architecture decisions, not chores. The Cfqby branch's CI fix (frontend prebuild before clippy, real icons) folds into the new workflows; the branch itself is superseded.

kp_seam: Two generic core modules, zero Knowledge-Plane-specific code. (1) export/: a "markdown vault" is any configured directory. Pipeline per article: sanitized HTML → htmd DOM-walk → CommonMark, frontmatter rendered from a minijinja template (default fields: title, author, source URL, feed, published, saved, tags, curio_id [UUIDv7], checksum [sha256 of normalized body], schema_version) — the checksum field deliberately mirrors RFC-0018's frontmatter checksum-for-dedupe/incremental-reindex key, and the default schema is a near-drop-in for the KP inbox note contract. Idempotency via a manifest (SQLite table + sidecar .curio/manifest.json in the vault): curio_id → vault-relative path + last checksum, so re-promotion updates in place, renames are tracked, and same-title collisions get deterministic slug suffixes instead of silent overwrites. Writes are atomic (tmp+rename) because the vault is expected to be git- or watcher-observed mid-write; filename templates are strictly path-sanitized; images optionally copied as vault-relative assets. Per-feed/per-tag routing rules choose subdirectories. Surfaces: one-keystroke "promote" in the desktop head, `curio export` in the CLI, identical output from both. (2) events/: every state change appends to the events table; `curio events tail` / a configured file sink projects it as JSONL — {schema_version, event: subscribed|read|starred|read_later|promoted, article: {curio_id, url, title, feed}, ts} — a generic reading-signals stream any downstream consumes. Both schemas are published, semver'd JSON Schema artifacts from curio-types: they become public API the moment one external consumer exists, and the KP is that consumer. KP integration is therefore configuration only: point Curio's vault export at the KP vault's inbox/ (or a git-synced copy), point the librarian's interest model at the JSONL sink; the KP ingestion plane sees ordinary normalized markdown notes with checksums, exactly what RFC-0018 §Ingestion already specifies. The derived/disposable contract holds by design: a promoted note carries everything downstream needs, Curio's own SQLite can be wiped without losing anything canonical, and future annotations/highlights are specified to land in the vault as file edits, never in the app DB.
sync_story: Honest v1: no sync. Curio v1 is a single-machine desktop app + CLI over one SQLite file; there is no state-sync protocol, no cloud, no multi-device read-state, and the docs say so plainly. What crosses devices in v1 is exactly two things: promoted articles (markdown in the vault — durable via whatever syncs the vault, git in Alex's case) and the event stream (JSONL a downstream can ship anywhere). The architecture makes the two credible future answers cheap without committing to either: (a) curio-server head — the same core crate behind axum exposing GReader + Fever server APIs, letting the existing healthy OSS mobile ecosystem (Capy Reader, Reeder Classic, Unread, NewsFlash) attach to a self-hosted Curio instance; the CLI head's daily CI proves the core already runs headless, so this is API surface work, not a rewrite; (b) client mode — a StateBackend port in core with a GReader/Fever/Miniflux client implementation, making the desktop a cockpit over an existing FreshRSS/Miniflux/Feedbin account and inheriting their mobile apps. The event-sourced article_state model is the enabling decision for both: read/star/read-later become replayable, mergeable event streams rather than mutable flags, so bidirectional reconciliation is a deterministic merge instead of a retrofit. Neither head ships in v1; the seams (head-agnostic core, event-sourced state, published schemas) are the v1 deliverable. For Alex's KP couch/mobile reach specifically: promotion-to-vault-via-git is the v1 answer (per raw/curio-rss.md's own candidate resolution), and the G11 ingress tier can later front a curio-server instance if that head gets built.
code_reuse: Roughly 35–40% of the Rust and the best of the frontend/DX survive, relocated into the workspace. Ported near-verbatim into curio-core: error.rs (three-tier model, the branch's best module), the serde model layer (minus the duplicate Theme structs and aspirational no-op settings), the SQLite DDL as the basis of migration 0001 (amended: INTEGER rowid alias + UUIDv7, article_state/events split, single RFC3339 date format, FTS trigger scoped to content changes), core/feeds/parser.rs (feed-rs usage + tests, with content_text population and site_url/date fixes added), core/feeds/opml.rs (curio:* round-trip, kept whole), the ammonia sanitizer config (finally wired into ingest; truncate_text fixed to char boundaries), the YouTube/Reddit URL parsers with their genuinely thorough tests, and themes.rs data converted to JSON assets. services/refresh.rs survives as the design of pipeline/ (bounded concurrency, conditional GET, per-feed isolation) — its code is absorbed and fixed (async-safe store calls, etag preservation on error), and it becomes the only fetch path, deleting the sequential command duplicate. Ported into apps/desktop: the entire app.css token system + 9 themes, AddFeedModal's dialog a11y pattern, the Reddit/YouTube viewer components as visual reference (rewritten to runes once view-mode dispatch exists), format.ts + its tests, the three-column shell layout as a wireframe. DX kept: lefthook config, .vscode set, Makefile task surface (recast as justfile/xtask), CI bones including the webkit-deps and frontend-prebuild-before-clippy solutions (folding in the Cfqby branch's fix; that branch is otherwise superseded), the dependency-review gate. Rewritten from scratch: storage access layer (pool/writer-thread repo), all refresh/feed commands (thin shells over core), markdown.rs (htmd DOM conversion), export.rs (manifest-driven exporter + event log), settings validation, favicon service (keep the ico→link-rel strategy, drop the block_on code, Google fallback opt-in), the entire frontend state layer and shell/keyboard/dispatch, tauri.conf + capabilities. Dropped: platform/podcast dead structs, player.ts, the 33MB committed yt-dlp binary, committed gen/ output, ts-rs scaffolding (replaced by tauri-specta), husky remnants, unused deps (mockall/tokio-test/image). The 139 existing unit tests ride along with their modules as the regression floor for what they actually cover.
risks: Boundary erosion: Tauri/UI types leaking into curio-core would quietly kill the whole thesis — must be mechanically enforced (cargo-deny ban on tauri in core's dep tree + the CLI head building in CI daily), not policed by review

Async-over-rusqlite facade (writer thread + read pool + spawn_blocking) is subtle to get right; deadlocks/starvation under refresh load are the failure mode — needs its own stress tests, and the fallback decision point (switch to sqlx/tokio-rusqlite) should be pre-agreed rather than fought mid-crisis

tauri-specta version coupling: the binding generator tracks Tauri 2.x releases imperfectly; a Tauri upgrade can strand codegen — pin versions, keep bindings committed so builds never depend on generator availability

Rewrite scope is larger than 'salvage-parts' sounds: storage access, pipeline, export, and the entire frontend state layer are from-scratch; the validation report's Phase-1-~70% optimism must not anchor effort estimates

Publishing the frontmatter/event schemas early freezes them — the KP becomes a consumer immediately, so a v0 mistake (e.g. wrong identity key) is a breaking change with a real downstream; schema review before first KP wiring is load-bearing
curio-server scope creep: GReader/Fever server compatibility is a rabbit hole of client quirks; the workspace stub invites contributors to demand it — governance must hold it post-v1 explicitly

Enrichment reputation risk survives the feature flags: users judge the product on Reddit/YouTube flakiness even when clearly labeled best-effort; ToS posture (Reddit JSON) needs documenting before an OSS launch invites scrutiny

Cross-platform webview variance (WebKitGTK codecs/a11y on Linux vs WebView2) lands entirely on the desktop head's QA matrix; without the documented manual per-OS pass, Linux ships broken audio/a11y silently

Signing/notarization operational burden (Apple Developer ID custody, Windows cert, updater key rotation/compromise plan) is real recurring cost decided at first release and near-impossible to change later
'Curio' name/trademark collision (Zengobi Curio et al.) and the baked-in bundle identifier must clear before any public artifact — both are effectively immutable afterward

Single-maintainer sustainability: the architecture multiplies surfaces (core + 2-3 heads + published schemas); mitigation is the documented anyone-can-cut-a-release pipeline and an explicit personal-first governance statement, but the risk is structural

name: server-first — "curiod + cockpit" (embedded-or-remote Rust server as the single source of truth)
summary: Curio becomes two artifacts built from one Cargo workspace: `curiod`, a Miniflux-class self-hostable single Rust binary (axum + SQLite/WAL, embedded web UI, GReader + Fever compat APIs, native /v1 API with SSE), and the Tauri desktop "cockpit", a rich client that ALWAYS speaks the HTTP API — in standalone mode it embeds the identical curio-server crate in-process over a Unix socket/loopback, in remote mode it points at a self-hosted curiod. Sync is therefore by construction, not bolted on: there is exactly one state machine and one writer of truth, and "multi-device" is just a base URL — no second protocol, no reconciliation layer invented after state shapes ossify (the NetNewsWire-iCloud and new-Reeder failure modes). The flip also fixes the shipped branch's worst structural defects at the root: the fragile hand-typed Tauri IPC surface (the snake_case bug class) collapses into an OpenAPI-generated TS client; stored-XSS dies because sanitization happens once at ingest on the server; the vault export/event log (the Knowledge-Plane seam) becomes a server-side, event-driven subsystem with named destinations instead of an arbitrary-path IPC file-write primitive. The cost — auth, HTTP hop, process lifecycle, a compat-protocol test matrix, doubled release targets — is paid deliberately because it buys the homelab community's proven adoption pattern (FreshRSS/Miniflux + the existing OSS mobile-client ecosystem) plus a genuinely excellent desktop app the ecosystem lacks, and because it makes real browser-based E2E testing possible where the pure-Tauri design structurally could not.

key_decisions.decision: One state machine: curiod is the only writer of truth; the desktop app is always an API client, embedding the identical curio-server crate in-process for standalone mode (UDS/loopback + one-time token), or pointing at a remote instance.
key_decisions.rationale: Sync-by-construction beats sync-bolted-on because there is never a second copy of state to reconcile: remote vs local differs only in base URL, so every feature (read state, counts, search, export) works identically on day one across web UI, desktop, and mobile compat clients. Bolted-on sync means inventing a reconciliation protocol after the local schema ossifies — the documented NetNewsWire-iCloud multi-hour-sync and new-Reeder sync-removal failures. Costs accepted: an HTTP hop locally (µs over UDS), auth surface, embedded-server lifecycle in Tauri, and a doubled release matrix — each mitigated by a named decision below.

key_decisions.decision: API boundary: HTTP/JSON + SSE change feed; OpenAPI 3.1 derived from the curio-api DTO crate (utoipa); TS client generated into packages/api-client, committed, and CI-diffed; errors as RFC-9457 problem+json carrying the branch's error-code/recoverable contract. Tauri IPC shrinks to ~6 shell-only commands with a real capabilities file.
key_decisions.rationale: The shipped branch's fatal snake_case-vs-camelCase IPC bug and hand-typed drifting TS types are a structural consequence of an unverified hand-maintained boundary; codegen from one Rust contract crate eliminates the class. Shrinking Tauri IPC to window/opener/dialog/keychain glue means the entire application is testable without a webview, and the frontend runs unmodified in a plain browser.

key_decisions.decision: Storage: SQLite only (no Postgres option), WAL + busy_timeout, a single-writer actor task + read-connection pool behind spawn_blocking; articles get INTEGER PRIMARY KEY with UUIDv7 as a unique column; content/state split into articles (immutable content), article_state (mutable flags), and an append-only events table; dedupe chain guid→link→hash(title+published); all timestamps INTEGER unix-millis; forward-only per-transaction migrations owned exclusively by curio-store.
key_decisions.rationale: Fixes every catalogued storage defect at once: global Mutex serialization and async-blocking (writer actor + read pool), no-transaction 2N-statement upserts (one tx + INSERT..ON CONFLICT per refresh), the FTS5 external-content rowid-instability hazard on TEXT PKs (INTEGER pk), UUIDv4 insert-locality (v7), date-format schizophrenia (integers), and retention interacting with starred/promoted items (state table + rules in core). SQLite-not-Postgres is deliberate positioning: Miniflux's #1 adoption complaint is the Postgres requirement; Readeck proved one-binary+SQLite is the homelab sweet spot.

key_decisions.decision: The append-only events table is first-class infrastructure, written in the same transaction as every state mutation (subscribed/read/starred/read-later/promoted).
key_decisions.rationale: One mechanism powers four features: the SSE live-update feed to clients, incremental sync cursors (GET /v1/events?since=), the JSONL reading-behavior export the Knowledge Plane consumes, and the feed-intelligence dashboard (read-ratio, never-read pruning). No competitor exposes reading behavior as user-owned data; here it falls out of the write path for free.

key_decisions.decision: Ship GReader API + Fever API compatibility endpoints in curio-server at launch, alongside the richer native /v1.
key_decisions.rationale: This is the homelab adoption lever and the honest mobile story: Capy Reader, Reeder Classic, Unread, FeedMe, and NewsFlash all speak these protocols today, so a self-hosted curiod instantly has mature mobile clients on both platforms without Curio building any. It is the exact pattern (FreshRSS/Miniflux as sync hub) the OSS ecosystem already organizes around — Curio joins as both a better hub (vault export, events, rich views) and the missing first-class desktop client. Cost: a real compat-test matrix, budgeted in the testing architecture.

key_decisions.decision: Content pipeline runs once, server-side, at ingest: ammonia sanitize (branch config kept) → text extraction populating content_text → optional readability full-content fetch per feed; HTML→Markdown is DOM-based via htmd, proven against the fixture corpus. Raw upstream HTML is never stored or served.
key_decisions.rationale: Kills the branch's critical stored-XSS chain at the root — safety no longer depends on every renderer remembering to sanitize — and makes FTS real (the branch indexes NULL content today). The regex Markdown converter that destroys multi-line elements is replaced on the exact seam the Knowledge-Plane export depends on. Server-side means web UI, desktop, and mobile compat clients all benefit identically.

key_decisions.decision: Security model: server side — argon2 password + API tokens + optional reverse-proxy header auth, bind-localhost default with explicit --listen, SSRF deny-private/loopback-by-default on ALL outbound fetches, response size/redirect caps, XML entity limits; client side — strict CSP in both shells (default-src 'self'; frame-src youtube-nocookie click-to-load only), minimal Tauri capabilities, no shell plugin, scoped opener; vault-export destinations are NAMED server-side configuration, never arbitrary paths over the wire.
key_decisions.rationale: Server-first raises the stakes — a feed-fetching daemon on a LAN is an SSRF pivot, and auth bugs are remote — so guardrails the desktop design could defer become v1-blocking here, priced in. The destination registry structurally removes the branch's XSS→export_to_markdown(path)→arbitrary-file-write primitive: the API can only say 'promote article X to destination named Y'.

key_decisions.decision: Platform enrichment is server-side, trait-ported (RedditEnricher/YouTubeEnricher in curio-enrich), feature-gated at compile time AND runtime-optional: Reddit public JSON best-effort behind a circuit breaker with honest degraded-mode UI and optional user-supplied OAuth creds; yt-dlp NEVER bundled or auto-updated inside the app — external managed binary, SHA256-pinned download into the data dir, `--` argv separation, strict URL validation; both degrade silently to RSS-only rendering.
key_decisions.rationale: Bundled auto-updating yt-dlp breaks macOS notarization, Flathub, and distro policy, and its argv handling on the branch is an RCE vector; Reddit's post-2023 throttling makes enrichment unreliable by nature. Traits keep rich views as the differentiator (Folo's 36k stars prove demand) while making the two highest-breakage dependencies removable without touching core. Server-side execution also centralizes rate-limit budgets instead of N clients hammering endpoints.

key_decisions.decision: One frontend, two shells: a single SvelteKit app in Svelte 5 runes with TanStack Query + SSE invalidation, server-computed unread counts, virtualized article lists, a keyboard-shortcut registry, and per-feed view-mode dispatch — built once, wrapped by Tauri and rust-embed'd into curiod as the web UI.
key_decisions.rationale: Self-hosters expect a web UI (every incumbent has one); building it costs near-zero since the desktop frontend is already a web app under this architecture. Query-cache + SSE structurally fixes the branch's wrong-badges-derived-from-page-1 and refetch-tree-per-click bugs, and one codebase avoids the Electron-vs-web split that kills solo-maintained readers.

key_decisions.decision: Testing architecture mapped to crates: property/unit tests in curio-core (proptest on dedupe, slugs, retention); nasty-corpus + cargo-fuzz on feed parsing and HTML→Markdown in curio-content; migration tests against committed fixture DBs from every released version in curio-store; full-server in-process integration tests (axum + temp SQLite + wiremock upstream feeds) as the workhorse tier; REAL Playwright E2E driving apps/frontend against the actual curiod binary in a browser; tauri-driver smoke on Linux/Windows only; a scripted GReader/Fever compat matrix against real client releases; criterion benches + the seeded 50k-article dataset as CI perf gates.
key_decisions.rationale: The branch's test suite validated what was easy and missed every load-bearing defect because the riskiest seam (client↔backend) was untestable Tauri IPC. Server-first makes that seam plain HTTP: the PRD's incoherent Playwright-drives-Tauri strategy becomes straightforwardly implementable, and integration tests exercise the same binary users deploy.

key_decisions.decision: Performance strategy for 500+ feeds / 50k+ articles: one transaction per feed refresh with ON CONFLICT upserts; FTS triggers that fire only on content change (never on read-flag flips); keyset pagination everywhere (no OFFSET); maintained count aggregates; SSE deltas instead of poll-refetch; UDS transport locally; targets (<1s first paint of first page, instant FTS at 100k items) verified by the fixtures/ dataset generator + criterion in CI on defined runner hardware — published as a benchmark page.
key_decisions.rationale: The dossier shows the branch fails at scale by construction (sequential refresh, per-article autocommit fsyncs, O(n) pagination, FTS churn on hot writes). Performance is a stated differentiator vs Electron/PHP incumbents, so it gets a falsifiable harness, not aspirational metrics.

key_decisions.decision: Modes are explicit, never magic: a profile is either 'standalone' (embedded server, DB in the OS data dir) or 'remote' (URL + token from the keychain). Migrating standalone→self-hosted is copying the SQLite file to the server (same binary, same schema, same migrations) or `curio export --all` + import. Peer-to-peer merge of two standalone instances is explicitly out of scope.
key_decisions.rationale: The embedded/remote symmetry gives an honest upgrade path the desktop-only design can never offer — your local instance IS a server already — while refusing the CRDT/merge swamp keeps v1 shippable. Users who want serverless multi-device get a documented answer (run curiod; or wait for the post-v1 sync-journal exploration) instead of a silently corrupting one.

key_decisions.decision: Packaging and OSS hygiene: curiod ships as a static binary + Docker image + systemd unit with SBOM and minisign checksums; desktop ships signed/notarized via tauri-action with tauri-plugin-updater (updater disabled in distro-managed packages); dual MIT/Apache-2.0 license decided pre-launch; cargo-deny license+advisory gates enforced; CI fork-safe (conditional signing, SHA-pinned actions); no telemetry, PRIVACY.md enumerating every possible network call.
key_decisions.rationale: The landscape shows trust-positioning ('cannot be acquihired away, data on disk, boring deps') is a rational purchase criterion post-Omnivore/Pocket; release engineering is most of production cost and the dossier shows the branch's release workflow is not shippable — so the pipeline is architecture, not an afterthought.

kp_seam: The seam is curio-export, a server-side subsystem with two products — vault export and event log — both generic, neither Obsidian- nor Knowledge-Plane-branded. (1) Vault export: the server config declares named 'markdown vault destinations' — v1 kind: a directory on the host where curiod runs (which in standalone mode IS the user's machine, so the desktop story is unchanged); a git-push destination kind (clone/commit/push a remote repo) is the designed follow-up that makes remote-server deployments first-class. Promotion is an API action (`POST /v1/articles/{id}/promote {destination}`) bound to one keystroke in the reader; the pipeline is: stored sanitized HTML → readability body → htmd DOM Markdown → template-rendered YAML frontmatter (minijinja templates, user-overridable per destination) → atomic tmp+rename write (the vault may be git-watched mid-write). Frontmatter is schema-versioned and carries: title, author, source URL, feed, published, saved, tags, a stable `curio_id` (UUIDv7), and a `checksum` of the rendered content — deliberately field-compatible with RFC-0018's ingestion contract (title/source/tags/status/checksum for dedupe + incremental reindex), but expressed in docs as 'works with any Obsidian/Logseq/plain-git vault'. Re-promotion is idempotent: the exporter locates the existing note by curio_id in frontmatter and updates in place; filename collisions resolve by slug + curio_id suffix; images optionally copy as vault-relative assets. Promotion emits a `promoted` event and the note carries everything downstream needs — Curio's DB remains derived/disposable under the KP canonical/derived rule (wiping curiod loses no promoted knowledge), and annotations are explicitly a vault-side concern, never app-DB state. (2) Event log: the append-only events table (written transactionally with every state change) exports as JSONL — `{schema_version, event: subscribed|read|starred|read_later_added|read_later_removed|promoted, article_id (curio_id), url, feed, ts}` — to configured sinks: a rotating file path, a webhook, plus `GET /v1/events?since=<cursor>` and `curio events tail` for pull consumers. Both schemas (frontmatter + events) are versioned, documented public API with a stated breaking-change policy, because the moment one external consumer exists they are load-bearing. Alex's deployment is then pure configuration, zero Curio code: curiod runs as an always-on guest (G11 tier per RFC-0017/ADR-0029 posture), its vault destination points at the KP vault's git-mirrored `inbox/` (via the git-push destination), its JSONL sink lands where the librarian's maintenance loop reads, and the interest model consumes the event stream — while every OSS user sees only 'markdown vault export + reading-signals log'.
sync_story: Honest tiering. Tier 1 (v1, the real answer): run curiod anywhere — a Pi, an LXC, a NAS, Docker — and every surface shares live state by construction: the desktop cockpit (remote mode), the embedded web UI from any browser/couch device, and existing mobile apps via the GReader/Fever compat APIs (Capy Reader and ReadYou on Android; Reeder Classic, Unread, NetNewsWire on iOS/macOS). Curio ships zero mobile apps and does not need to: it speaks the protocols the healthy OSS mobile ecosystem already builds against. Tier 2 (v1, standalone): the desktop app with its embedded server is fully offline-complete on one machine — all text and images local, usable on a plane — with a documented one-step upgrade to Tier 1 (move the SQLite file; same binary, same schema). What v1 does NOT do, stated plainly: no sync between two standalone desktop installs (no CRDT, no merge — if you want two devices, run the server); remote-mode desktop offline support is a client-side read cache of already-fetched articles/images plus a small replay outbox for idempotent state flips (mark-read/star) with server-side last-write-wins on reconnect — it is NOT full offline parity, and new fetches require connectivity; no hosted service, no E2EE relay, no iCloud/Syncthing file sync (SQLite over file-sync silently corrupts; the docs say so loudly and the server refuses to open a DB on known network filesystems without an override flag). Post-v1 exploration, explicitly not promised: a file-based event-journal sync for the serverless crowd, feasible only because the events table already exists as an append-only substrate.
code_reuse: Roughly 35-40% of the branch survives, redistributed across the workspace. Survives with minor fixes: error.rs three-tier design → curio-api's problem+json contract (codes + recoverable flag kept, IPC serialization replaced); the SQLite schema shape (FKs, FTS5 external-content protocol, indexes) → curio-store migration v1 with the decided fixes (INTEGER pk, state/content split, unix-ms timestamps); core serde models (Article/Feed/Folder/Settings, filter/pagination types) → curio-core (duplicate Theme structs deduped, no-op settings deleted or implemented); feed-rs parser + its tests → curio-content (content_text population added, site_url link-rel fix, published fallback); the OPML state machine with the curio:* extension namespace → curio-server import/export, kept nearly verbatim (its round-trip tests are the best in the repo); the ammonia sanitizer config → curio-content ingest (finally wired; truncate_text rewritten on char boundaries); services/refresh.rs's design (bounded buffer_unordered, 30s timeouts, conditional GET, per-feed error isolation, broadcast shutdown) → curio-fetch's scheduler as the single fetch path, with the etag-clobber and sync-DB-call defects fixed; YouTube/Reddit URL parsers + tests → curio-enrich; themes.rs data → JSON assets served by the server. Frontend: app.css token system + 9 themes carried whole; AddFeedModal's dialog a11y and error-unwrap patterns as the component template; Reddit/YouTube viewer components as visual/markup reference for the runes rewrite; format.ts + its 36 tests as-is. DX/CI: lefthook config, biome config, .vscode, Makefile task surface (→ just), the CI matrix skeleton with the Cfqby branch's clippy-frontend-prebuild fix folded in. Discarded outright: database.rs's access layer, the 46-command Tauri IPC surface and api/index.ts + hand-typed TS (replaced by ~6 shell commands + generated client), regex markdown.rs, favicon.rs, the dead platform/podcast module, player.ts and the Svelte-4-idiom stores, gen-types.sh, the 34MB committed yt-dlp binary, committed gen/ output, and the 'Phase-1 ~70%' claim as any planning input.
risks: Scope gravity: server-first adds auth, two deployment targets, compat protocols, and process-lifecycle code to an already-ambitious v1 — the single likeliest failure mode is joining the OSS-desktop graveyard by shipping nothing; mitigations (single-user-only v1, in-process embedded server, one frontend, enrichment feature-flagged off) must be enforced, not aspirational.

Desktop feel: every interaction crossing HTTP — even over UDS — plus a query-cache layer can make the cockpit feel like a web app in a frame; hitting the <50ms interaction bar requires optimistic updates + SSE done well, and this is exactly the polish a solo maintainer cuts under pressure.

GReader/Fever compat is a swamp of undocumented client-specific behaviors (clients are tested against FreshRSS/Miniflux quirks, not specs); subtle breakage in Reeder Classic or Capy Reader would poison the homelab launch narrative — the compat matrix needs real devices/clients in the release checklist, which is ongoing manual cost.

Security stakes rise structurally: a self-hosted feed fetcher is an SSRF pivot into the user's LAN and auth bugs are remote code-adjacent; the SSRF policy, bind-localhost defaults, and SECURITY.md are v1 blockers, and any early CVE hits exactly the trust-positioned audience hardest.

Embedded-server lifecycle on desktop has sharp edges: crashed background runtime, WAL locks after force-kill, OS sleep/wake, port/socket collisions, antivirus interference on Windows loopback — a class of bugs the pure-IPC design never had; needs a supervised startup/health-check/recovery path in the Tauri shell.

Remote-mode vault export writes on the SERVER's filesystem — users will intuitively expect notes on the machine they read on; until the git-push destination kind ships, remote deployments have an awkward vault story (NFS/syncthing workarounds), and git-push-to-vault brings its own auth/conflict surface.

Miniflux/FreshRSS comparison trap: announcing a self-hostable reader server invites day-one feature comparisons (multi-user, filtering rules maturity, extension ecosystems, years of hardening); positioning must stay 'the reading surface with a vault contract + the missing desktop client', or Curio fights incumbents on their strongest ground with a v1 feature set.

Last-write-wins on the remote-mode replay outbox will occasionally lose a read/star flip between devices; acceptable if documented, reputation-damaging if discovered — the events substrate should record enough to debug user reports.

htmd/readability-rs maturity carries the flagship export: the branch proved how badly this seam fails silently; the fixture corpus and idempotent-re-export tests are the actual insurance and must be built early, not after the first vault-corruption report.

Public API surface triples (native v1 + GReader + Fever + frontmatter schema + events schema) and each becomes a compat commitment the moment one external consumer exists — including the Knowledge Plane itself; versioning discipline and a breaking-change policy are needed from the first tagged release.

Naming/identifier debts from the dossier ('Curio' trademark collision, com.curio-reader.app identifier, no LICENSE file) are orthogonal to architecture but block the first public artifact; the identifier is near-immutable once an updater exists, so it must be fixed before any signed release.
